tools.name: inspect_promotion
tools.description: Quarantine declared exposure, compare paired baseline/candidate outcomes on the clean remainder, and issue a promotion receipt. Bring your own exam rows, exposure records, and per-item results. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'inspector'.

tools.name: audit_leakage
tools.description: Exact declared-exposure audit over your exam rows: row identity, behavioral fingerprints for graph-DSL expressions, text-similarity review flags, and a clean exam export. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'leakage'.

tools.name: promotion_gate
tools.description: PASS, HOLD, or BLOCK from paired per-item results: gains, regressions, exact McNemar p-value, per-domain breakdown. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'gate'.

tools.name: bank_health
tools.description: Item-lifecycle diagnostics over your grading history: discriminators, saturated and flaky items, frontier gaps. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'health'.

tools.name: safe_patch
tools.description: Apply a section-scoped Markdown patch under conservation checks (untouched sections stay byte-identical; protected tokens preserved). Full example: GET /api/examples key 'safepatch'.

tools.name: counterexample_hunt
tools.description: Bounded simulated-annealing search for a graph counterexample inside a DSL predicate class, with an exact certificate when found. CPU-bounded and strictly rate-limited. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'counterexample'.

tools.name: memory_relevance
tools.description: Compare query-free salience against objective-conditioned relevance for a set of memories under a token budget. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'memory'.

tools.name: replay_trace
tools.description: Turn reasoning-emulator control events into checkpoints, rewinds, notes, and a timeline. Full example: GET /api/examples key 'replay'.

tools.name: report_card_start
tools.description: TIER 1: start a disposable report-card session. Returns exam items (graph-repair prompts minted from the repository's public frontier) for THIS agent to answer. Answer every item, then call report_card_submit exactly once. Sessions are one-shot, expire in 15 minutes, and are strictly rate-limited. T

tools.name: report_card_submit
tools.description: TIER 1: submit answers for a report-card session and receive the graded report (per-item verdicts, per-domain totals, SHA-256 commitments). Grading is by checker spec: any verified strict refinement of the item's predicate passes; no answer key exists. The session is destroyed by this call.

tools.name: about_whetstone
tools.description: What this service is: the tool catalog, the tier boundaries, and where the source lives.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: inspect_promotion, audit_leakage · Private data: inspect_promotion, safe_patch, memory_relevance, report_card_start · Public sink: report_card_submit.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: inspect_promotion, audit_leakage · Private data: inspect_promotion, safe_patch, memory_relevance, report_card_start · Public sink: report_card_submit

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
